subtopic: Seventh Schedule, territorial nexus, Union intervention, repugnancy, directions and All India Services

content.issueStory: Indian federalism distributes ordinary authority yet creates constitutionally specified routes for Union legislation or direction when national, emergency or coordination needs arise. Articles 245-263 and the Seventh Schedule allocate legislative competence and administrative coordination. Parliament may enter State subjects through specified constitutional routes. Repugnancy under Article 254 concerns conflicting Union and State law in the Concurrent field, subject to constitutional rules.
content.coreConcept: Articles 245-263 and the Seventh Schedule allocate legislative competence and administrative coordination. Parliament may enter State subjects through specified constitutional routes. Repugnancy under Article 254 concerns conflicting Union and State law in the Concurrent field, subject to constitutional rules.
dimensions: Union, State and Concurrent Lists distribute subject competence, with residuary power at the Union.
Territorial nexus and extra-territorial rules govern legislative reach.
Parliament may legislate on State subjects through Rajya Sabha resolution, emergency, State consent, treaty implementation or President’s Rule contexts.
Article 254 regulates repugnancy in the Concurrent field and includes presidential-consideration rules for State laws.
Union directions and executive-power correspondence structure administrative relations.
All India Services and intergovernmental mechanisms combine national standards with State administration.
content.dataReport: Uploaded source basis: STATIC_09_Polity.pdf and PYQ_06_Polity_Governance.pdf. Refresh amendments, statutory rules, office status and later judgments from official sources: Constitution of India, India Code and Inter-State Council Secretariat.
content.caseStudy: Article 249 uses a Rajya Sabha national-interest resolution to temporarily authorise Parliament on a State List matter without permanently transferring the subject.
argumentsAgainst: Broad Union powers can centralise practice.
Administrative directions can generate political conflict.
content.pyqLink: UPSC patterns test Article-institution matches, exceptions, procedure and comparative statements within Legislative and Administrative Centre-State Relations. Resolve each statement through source, scope, procedure and consequence.

answerFramework.intro: Introduce Legislative and Administrative Centre-State Relations through the controlling constitutional principle and legal source.
body: Map the authority: Articles 245-263, Seventh Schedule, Articles 249-253, 254, 256-257 and 312.

Explain the first-order design: Union, State and Concurrent Lists distribute subject competence, with residuary power at the Union. Territorial nexus and extra-territorial rules govern legislative reach. Parliament may legislate on State subjects through Rajya Sabha resolution, emergency, State consent, treaty implementation or President’s Rule contexts.

Analyse safeguards and tensions: Article 254 regulates repugnancy in the Concurrent field and includes presidential-consideration rules for State laws. Union directions and executive-power correspondence structure administrative relations. All India Services and intergovernmental mechanisms combine national standards with State administration.

Use Article 249 uses a Rajya Sabha national-interest resolution to temporarily authorise Parliament on a State List matter without permanently transferring the subject. and close with constitutional accountability plus dated legal verification.
answerFramework.conclusion: A strong Polity answer joins constitutional text, institutional purpose, judicial doctrine and a workable accountability safeguard.

mainsExamples: Parliament acting under Article 252 after State resolutions.
prelimsFacts: Residuary legislative power lies with Parliament.
Article 252 begins with consenting State legislatures.
Repugnancy primarily concerns Concurrent List conflict.
All India Services are addressed by Article 312.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the controlling rule in Legislative and Administrative Centre-State Relations?
revisionBox.answer: Articles 245-263 and the Seventh Schedule allocate legislative competence and administrative coordination. Parliament may enter State subjects through specified constitutional routes. Repugnancy under Article 254 concerns conflicting Union and State law in the Concurrent field, subject to constitutional rules.

revisionBox.prompt: What is the primary constitutional or legal authority?
What is the fastest elimination trap?
revisionBox.answer: Do not apply Article 254 repugnancy mechanically to every Union-State conflict.

revisionBox.prompt: Where should later legal changes be verified?
revisionBox.answer: Constitution of India, India Code and Inter-State Council Secretariat

story: Indian federalism distributes ordinary authority yet creates constitutionally specified routes for Union legislation or direction when national, emergency or coordination needs arise.
core_concept: Articles 245-263 and the Seventh Schedule allocate legislative competence and administrative coordination. Parliament may enter State subjects through specified constitutional routes. Repugnancy under Article 254 concerns conflicting Union and State law in the Concurrent field, subject to constitutional rules.
mnemonic: L-R-S-A: Lists, Routes, Supremacy rules, Administration.

branches.label: Text
children: Union, State and Concurrent Lists distribute subject competence, with residuary power at the Union.
Territorial nexus and extra-territorial rules govern legislative reach.

branches.label: Institution
children: Parliament may legislate on State subjects through Rajya Sabha resolution, emergency, State consent, treaty implementation or President’s Rule contexts.
Article 254 regulates repugnancy in the Concurrent field and includes presidential-consideration rules for State laws.

branches.label: Safeguard
children: Union directions and executive-power correspondence structure administrative relations.
All India Services and intergovernmental mechanisms combine national standards with State administration.

branches.label: Traps
children: Do not apply Article 254 repugnancy mechanically to every Union-State conflict.
Do not say Article 249 permanently amends the State List.
Do not confuse legislative and executive distribution.

Article 252 begins with consenting State legislatures.
Repugnancy primarily concerns Concurrent List conflict.
All India Services are addressed by Article 312.

Do not say Article 249 permanently amends the State List.
Do not confuse legislative and executive distribution.

pyq_refs.question: PYQ pattern: constitutional statement testing on Legislative and Administrative Centre-State Relations.
pyq_refs.answer: Check text, scope, exception and competent authority.
